focus: Q1+Q2+Q4-light: spawn-site audit, send_bytes audit, log-history reconstruction

question: Q1
title: start_daemon is the only direct spawn site, but reachable concurrently from 8 callers with no inter-process lock
recommendation: Add fcntl.flock-style advisory lock on daemon.pid inside start_daemon to handle multi-process TOCTOU.

question: Q4
title: _async_daemon_main unconditionally unlinks the socket on startup; this is the mechanism severing prior live daemons
recommendation: Add daemon-side guard: refuse to unlink if daemon.pid references a live PID; or fail-fast with clear error.

question: Q2
title: Non-streaming send_bytes at daemon.py:441 has no inner try; outer handler logs full traceback for every BrokenPipeError
recommendation: Wrap each daemon send_bytes with a helper that swallows BrokenPipeError/ConnectionResetError quietly, OR demote outer except to DEBUG for those types.

question: Q1
title: ensure_daemon version-mismatch path can spawn a duplicate daemon if stop_daemon does not actually terminate
recommendation: Verify _pid_alive(old_pid) is False after stop_daemon, with a hard timeout, before spawning.

question: Q6
title: Per-broken-pipe logger.exception traceback amplifies log growth (564 lines → 23MB)
recommendation: After bug-2 fix, add RotatingFileHandler config; demote BrokenPipeError logging to single-line WARN.

question: Q1
answer: Only start_daemon() directly spawns. 8 callers reach it; fixing it covers all. Adds: ensure_daemon version-mismatch path (P1-1) plus inter-process lock recommendation (P0-1).

question: Q2
answer: 6 daemon-side send_bytes call sites. Bug 2 covers 2 (the streaming double-crash pair). P0-3 raises a third (daemon.py:441 non-streaming reply).

question: Q4
answer: Mechanism is daemon.py:615 unlinking the socket file at startup. PID 98364 lost binding at 2026-04-27 17:08:49 (when PID 16404 spawned concurrently), NOT May 1 as spec.md hypothesized.
